steps.title: Decide if it is a statement
steps.content: The sentence gives a historical fact about South Carolina politician Robert C. De Large and the years 1865 to 1877. It is not asking a question. The clause after the comma begins with "which would," so "which" already functions as the subject of that relative clause.

steps.title: Test punctuation and subject
steps.content:
• Choice A - come to be known as the Reconstruction era?: This keeps the clause grammatical, but the question mark incorrectly turns a declarative statement into a question.
  • Choice C - it come to be known as the Reconstruction era.: Adding "it" creates a double subject because "which" is already the subject of "would come."
  • Choice D - it come to be known as the Reconstruction era?: This repeats the double-subject problem from C and also uses the wrong end punctuation.
The sentence needs no extra subject and should end with a period. The answer is B.
